compiled_state.title: External-test leak: enforce external classification + isolation safeguards in CI
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
An external (live Jira, real API mutation + cost) test LEAKED into a default run: the filter `-m "not integration"` does NOT exclude `external`, and the live tests run whenever Jira creds are present (skipif only gates on creds). This caused live mutations + orphan issues during routine suite runs. Research (recorded below) shows the fix is structural + env-gating, NOT an in-process network blocker (our external calls go through the `acli` SUBPROCESS, which no Python socket-patcher can catch).

## What
1. Env opt-in: external tests are INERT by default — require `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` (truthy) IN ADDITION to creds; a default run (even with creds) must not execute them. (Autouse/skip in tests/external/conftest.py.)
2. Collection-hook hygiene (root conftest pytest_collection_modifyitems): auto-apply `external` to everything under tests/external/; FAIL collection if an `external`-marked test lives outside tests/external/; assert exactly ONE tier marker per test (warn-mode env toggle for rollout).
3. `--strict-markers --strict-config` in pyproject; register all markers.
4. Fix the filter everywhere: default selection excludes ALL non-default tiers (`-m "not integration and not external"`), never just one. CI: a separate `external` job is the ONLY place with Jira creds + `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` + an `if:` fork-guard; default job has NO Jira creds.
5. Naming/dir clarity: confirm every test that makes EXTERNAL changes lives in tests/external/ with a clear `_live`/external name (reclassify any stray probe that exercises live behavior).
6. CI order-independence: run randomized (drop `-p no:randomly` in CI; seed printed for repro).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] External tests cannot run without REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL even with creds present (verified: default run collects 0 external)
- [ ] Collection hook FAILS on a misclassified/misplaced/over-tier test (verified with a deliberate violation)
- [ ] CI default job excludes integration+external and carries no Jira creds; external job is credential+opt-in scoped with a fork guard
- [ ] All current tests correctly classified; suite green; ruff clean

comments.body:
RESEARCH (cited) — patterns from actively-maintained Python projects:
- pytest-socket (--disable-socket --allow-hosts=127.0.0.1) blocks IN-PYTHON network; urllib3 uses it on every run; home-assistant/core in conftest. BUT it patches the parent interpreter's socket module ONLY — it does NOT catch our acli SUBPROCESS (separate process, own socket stack). Same limit for vcrpy/responses/respx/httpretty. So in-process blockers are insufficient for us.
- Subprocess-external defense: PATH-shadow a fake acli that hard-fails in the default tier (OpenFn/apollo monkeypatches subprocess.run/Popen to raise UnitTestViolation), + env-gate the real invocation, + credential-scope CI jobs. OS-level (--network none / unshare -n) is the only language-agnostic catch.
- Marker/dir hygiene (highest leverage): --strict-markers --strict-config (pandas). Collection hook pytest_collection_modifyitems auto-marks by directory and FAILS on misclassification: django-litefs conftest pytest.fail(pytrace=False) on zero/multiple/invalid tier markers (warn-mode env toggle for rollout); OpenFn/apollo + Barbora pytest-integration-mark auto-mark by path; pytest official recipe https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/example/markers.html.
- CI segregation: botocore runs only unit/functional (integration has NO public workflow); BottlecapDave/HomeAssistant-OctopusEnergy scopes secrets to the integration job env only + if: repository_owner==github.actor fork guard; aiohttp default addopts -m 'not autobahn and not dev_mode'.
- Order-independence: pytest-randomly (seeded, reproducible via --randomly-seed); run CI randomized (we currently use -p no:randomly). pytest-reverse / pytest-forked alternatives.
Bottom line: env opt-in (REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL) + external-dir confinement hook + credential-scoped CI = the real fix; pytest-socket is a useful add but would NOT have caught this subprocess leak.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Implemented external-test isolation. Env opt-in (REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL) autouse skip in tests/external/conftest.py; confinement hook in tests/conftest.py pytest_collection_modifyitems (auto-mark by dir + hard-fail external marker outside tests/external); --strict-markers --strict-config in pyproject addopts (all markers already registered, collects clean); separate credential+opt-in scoped external CI job with workflow_dispatch + owner==actor fork guard; meta-test tests/unit/test_external_isolation.py. Audit: zero stray live tests outside tests/external (all acli refs are mocked). Default run executes 0 external (1770 passed/7 skipped); integration 17 passed; deliberate violation fails collection; ruff clean.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
